prompt_verb_analysis.prompt_literal: Build me a tool that takes a GitHub username and shows me a developer profile card for them — their pinned repos, their contribution streak, their most-used language, and a small visualization of their commit activity for the last 90 days.
prompt_verb_analysis.rationale: 'build' refers to construction of the tool (AutoBuilder's job, not the user-facing verb). 'takes' is the input action. 'shows' is the user-observable verb that names what the tool does for the user: it shows a profile card with the four named elements. PNV.1 must therefore exercise opening the artifact, entering a username + PAT, submitting, and observing that all four card elements are shown.

prompt_named_verb_assertion.scenario: User double-clicks output/final/index.html in Windows Explorer (or opens via a trivial local static server if browser file:// security blocks fetch). The page loads without console errors. User types 'octocat' into the username field, pastes a valid GitHub PAT into the PAT field, clicks the lookup button. The page issues a GraphQL POST to https://api.github.com/graphql plus REST GETs to https://api.github.com/users/octocat and /users/octocat/repos (and per-repo /languages) under real network conditions with the real GitHub API responding.
prompt_named_verb_assertion.expected_result: A card is shown that contains, all visibly labeled: (a) octocat's avatar image, display name, and @octocat handle; (b) a 'Pinned repositories' section listing the pinned repos with name, description, language, and star count (or 'No pinned repositories' empty state); (c) a 'Current streak' figure in days; (d) a 'Most-used language across public repos' label with a language name; (e) a 'Contribution activity (last 90 days)' visualization showing 90 days of activity as a heatmap of 13 weeks x 7 days.

td_decisions_summary.deliverable_form: Single-folder static web page (output/final/index.html + css/ + js/ + research/). Pure ES modules loaded via <script type="module">. No build step. No CDN dependencies. No npm. No bundler.
td_decisions_summary.auth_strategy: User-supplied PAT (IP1 default branch). PAT held in memory for the session only — never persisted, never sent anywhere except api.github.com Authorization header.
td_decisions_summary.data_sources: GraphQL for pinnedItems + contributionsCollection (single query, requires PAT). REST /users/{login} for basic user info. REST /users/{login}/repos + per-repo /languages for byte-weighted language aggregation.
td_decisions_summary.streak_definition: Current consecutive-day streak ending at most-recent contribution day (per IP2 default).
td_decisions_summary.most_used_language: Bytes-weighted across owned non-fork public repos (per IP3 default / GitHub Linguist convention).
td_decisions_summary.ninety_day_viz: Heatmap strip 13 weeks x 7 days = 91 cells (90-day window padded to a clean grid), pure inline SVG, no charting library (per IP5 default).
td_decisions_summary.style: GitHub-native dark theme using GitHub color palette + system-ui font stack (per IP6 default). No web-font fetch, no Inter font download — system-ui covers the case at zero runtime dependency cost.
td_decisions_summary.rationale_for_5_sections: api-client (network + auth), data-derivers (pure metric computations), ui-shell (page chrome + form + status), card-renderer (DOM/SVG rendering of card), edge-case-testing (coverage). Each is small enough to be one logical builder pass and independent enough to be testable in isolation.

sections.name: GitHub API Client
sections.charter: Encapsulate all network interaction with api.github.com behind a single async function fetchProfilePayload(username, pat). Issue one GraphQL POST for pinnedItems + contributionsCollection (1-year window) and the REST calls for basic user info, owned non-fork repo list, and per-repo language byte breakdowns. Classify every failure into one of {UserNotFoundError, AuthError, RateLimitError, NetworkError, MissingTokenError} and throw a typed error rather than returning a partial payload silently. No DOM access. No global state.
sections.acceptance: fetchProfilePayload returns a fully-shaped ProfilePayload (per contracts/original/api-client--data-derivers.json) for a valid username + PAT. For an invalid username it throws UserNotFoundError. For a bad PAT it throws AuthError. For a 403 with X-RateLimit-Remaining: 0 it throws RateLimitError. For a fetch network failure it throws NetworkError. If pat is null, it returns a degraded payload with empty pinned_repos and empty contribution calendar (does NOT throw — that's MissingTokenError only when the caller explicitly required graph data).
out_of_scope: Retry/backoff logic (single attempt per call; rate-limit error message instructs user to retry)

sections.name: Metric Derivation Functions
sections.charter: Implement three pure functions: computeCurrentStreak(days), computeMostUsedLanguage(repos), and sliceLast90Days(days, today_utc). Each takes plain data shapes from the api-client payload and returns a derived result. No I/O. No DOM. No fetch. No timers. Fully deterministic.
sections.acceptance: Given the shapes specified in contracts/original/data-derivers--card-renderer.json, each function returns the documented result. computeCurrentStreak respects the 'today-with-zero does not break the streak yet' rule. computeMostUsedLanguage excludes forks and ties-break alphabetically. sliceLast90Days always returns exactly 90 entries (padding with zero days on the leading edge if necessary).
out_of_scope: Reading from GitHub API directly (consumes shapes only)

sections.name: Page Shell, Form, and Status
sections.charter: Build index.html with the static page chrome: page header/title, a username input, a PAT input (type=password), a lookup button, a status/error region, and a card container. Wire submit (button click or Enter key in username field) to call api-client.fetchProfilePayload, then hand the payload to card-renderer. On error, render the matching message from the error_handling_mapping in contracts/original/ui-shell--api-client.json. Include a visible link to GitHub's PAT creation page (FC.3). Handle repeat lookups in place (FC.10) without page reload.
sections.acceptance: Opening output/final/index.html in a modern browser shows the page with username input, PAT input + PAT-creation link, and lookup button visible above the fold. Pressing Enter in the username field triggers the same lookup as clicking the button. After a successful lookup, the card area is populated; a second lookup with a different username replaces the card content in place. Each error_type from the api-client throws maps to its documented UI message.
out_of_scope: Persistence of username/PAT in localStorage (per A10)
Theme toggle (single dark theme per IP6)
Mobile-specific responsive breakpoints beyond what falls out of a simple max-width centered layout

sections.name: Profile Card Renderer
sections.charter: Render the profile card from a ProfilePayload + derived metrics into #card-container. Layout: avatar + name + handle header; pinned-repos grid (up to 6 cards, name/description/language dot + name/star count); a stats row showing 'Current streak: N days', 'Most-used language across public repos: <language>', total contributions for the year; and a 90-day contribution-activity heatmap rendered as pure inline SVG (13 weeks x 7 days = 91 cells, GitHub-style green intensity scale, with the rightmost column = current week). Every metric self-labels per FC.9. Empty pinned_repos renders a 'No pinned repositories' state (FC.8). All user-controlled strings written via textContent (never innerHTML) to prevent XSS.
sections.acceptance: Given a valid ProfilePayload and derived metrics, the card renders all five elements (avatar+name+handle, pinned section, streak label, language label, heatmap SVG). Heatmap SVG contains exactly 91 <rect> cells. Heatmap intensity has 5 levels (0, 1-3, 4-6, 7-9, 10+ contributions) using GitHub's documented green palette. Streak label literally contains the string 'Current streak'. Language label literally contains the string 'Most-used language'. Heatmap caption literally contains the string 'Contribution activity (last 90 days)'.
out_of_scope: Sharing/exporting the card as an image

sections.name: Edge-Case Testing
sections.charter: Cover every acceptance assertion with `verifier: edge_case_testing` from the section list, exercised under production-fidelity environment per CV charter Section: Production-fidelity exercise — no substitution of runtime dependencies the artifact loads in production. Specifically: open output/final/index.html in a real browser (file:// or local static server), execute real fetch calls against api.github.com with a real PAT, and visually inspect the rendered card. Cover both successful flows (known-good usernames like octocat, torvalds, gaearon) and the error cases (nonexistent username, bad PAT, missing PAT, simulated network failure via DevTools offline mode).
sections.acceptance: Every MCA.* and DCA.* assertion in this file is exercised at least once. Each test produces a pass/fail recorded against the assertion id. Production-fidelity is documented: the test report names the actual browser version, OS, and GitHub API endpoints hit.
out_of_scope: Automated headless browser tests (Playwright/Puppeteer) — manual exercise under real browser is sufficient for v1 INLINE-mode build
Cross-browser matrix beyond user's actual browser

machine_checkable_assertions.statement: api-client module exports an async function named fetchProfilePayload.
fetchProfilePayload('octocat', '<valid_pat>') resolves to a ProfilePayload whose user.login === 'octocat'.
fetchProfilePayload('this-user-definitely-does-not-exist-xyzqq', '<valid_pat>') rejects with an Error whose name === 'UserNotFoundError'.
fetchProfilePayload('octocat', 'ghp_definitely_invalid_token_xyz') rejects with an Error whose name === 'AuthError'.
fetchProfilePayload('octocat', null) returns a payload with pinned_repos = [] and contribution_calendar_year.days = [], and does NOT throw (degraded-but-non-fatal behavior per FC.6 alt-a).
The GraphQL request body sent by api-client includes both pinnedItems(first: 6, types: [REPOSITORY]) and contributionsCollection(from: ..., to: ...) selections.
The Authorization header on requests with a PAT uses the form 'Bearer <pat>'.
machine_checkable_assertions.expected_value: header value matches /^Bearer ghp_/ or /^Bearer github_pat_/

machine_checkable_assertions.statement: computeCurrentStreak([]) returns {streak_days: 0, anchor_date: null}.
computeCurrentStreak with the last 5 days [1,1,1,1,1] returns streak_days >= 5.

computeMostUsedLanguage with repos = [{is_fork:false, languages_bytes:{JavaScript: 1000, Python: 500}}, {is_fork:true, languages_bytes:{Ruby: 9999}}] returns language === 'JavaScript' (forks excluded).
computeMostUsedLanguage with no eligible repos returns {language: null, bytes: 0, share_pct: 0}.
sliceLast90Days output length === 90 for any input.
output/final/index.html contains an input element with id='username-input'.
output/final/index.html contains an input element with id='pat-input' and type='password'.
output/final/index.html contains a button with id='lookup-button'.
output/final/index.html contains an anchor element with href starting with 'https://github.com/settings/tokens' (PAT creation link).
Opening output/final/index.html in a modern browser produces zero uncaught exceptions in the console before any user interaction.
machine_checkable_assertions.expected_value: no errors or uncaught exceptions on page load

machine_checkable_assertions.statement: After a successful lookup, performing a second lookup with a different username updates #card-container without a page reload.
machine_checkable_assertions.expected_value: card content reflects second username; no full-page reload event fires

machine_checkable_assertions.statement: After rendering, #card-container contains an element with src equal to user.avatar_url.
After rendering, #card-container contains a section labeled 'Pinned' with one repo card per pinned_repos entry (or a 'No pinned repositories' empty state if zero).
machine_checkable_assertions.expected_value: child count === pinned_repos.length OR text content includes 'No pinned repositories'

machine_checkable_assertions.statement: After rendering, #card-container text content includes the literal string 'Current streak'.
After rendering, #card-container text content includes the literal string 'Most-used language'.
After rendering, #card-container text content includes the literal string 'Contribution activity (last 90 days)'.
After rendering, the heatmap SVG within #card-container contains exactly 91 <rect> cells (13 weeks x 7 days).

card-renderer writes all user-controlled strings (login, name, bio, repo names, descriptions) via textContent, not innerHTML — verified by source-level grep showing zero occurrences of '.innerHTML =' against user-data variables.
machine_checkable_assertions.expected_value: no assignment of user-supplied strings to innerHTML

machine_checkable_assertions.statement: Heatmap cells use a 5-level GitHub-style green intensity scale; intensity 0 (zero contributions) uses a distinct color from intensity 1+.
machine_checkable_assertions.expected_value: distinct fill values count === 5 (or 4 if no zero-contribution cells present in the period)

machine_checkable_assertions.statement: Every MCA.* and DCA.* assertion in this file is exercised at least once during edge-case-testing and recorded pass/fail.
machine_checkable_assertions.expected_value: set(exercised) >= set(all MCA.* and DCA.* ids)

machine_checkable_assertions.statement: Edge-case testing is performed against the real api.github.com endpoint with a real PAT (no mocks, no stubs, no fixtures substituted for the network layer the artifact loads in production).
machine_checkable_assertions.expected_value: report names browser, OS, and at least three real api.github.com URLs hit during the test

discovery_coverage_assertions.statement: The delivered tool accepts a GitHub username and renders a single profile card summarizing pinned repos, contribution streak, most-used language, and 90-day commit activity.
discovery_coverage_assertions.scenario: Open output/final/index.html, enter 'octocat' + valid PAT, click lookup.
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: A single card appears containing all four named elements (pinned repos, streak, most-used language, 90-day viz).

discovery_coverage_assertions.statement: Deliverable is a small self-contained static web page (HTML/CSS/JS) the user opens locally in a browser, querying github.com's public APIs at lookup time, no database, no login.
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Folder contains index.html + css + js + research only; no server code, no database files, no auth/session storage logic.

discovery_coverage_assertions.statement: Tool is a static web page openable as index.html (no build step required).
discovery_coverage_assertions.scenario: Run output/final/index.html through a browser directly.
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Page loads and renders without a build/bundle step. No package.json, no node_modules required for delivery.

discovery_coverage_assertions.statement: Input is treated as a GitHub login handle (string), not a numeric id or URL.
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Lookup succeeds against GET /users/torvalds.

discovery_coverage_assertions.statement: Only public data is shown; no OAuth or login flow exists in the artifact.
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Zero matches (no auth flow code).

discovery_coverage_assertions.statement: Card is rendered for one username at a time (single-target view).
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Exactly one card element, not a list of cards.

discovery_coverage_assertions.statement: Pinned repos section shows up to 6 repos sourced from GraphQL user.pinnedItems(types: [REPOSITORY]).
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Query string includes 'pinnedItems(first: 6, types: [REPOSITORY])'.

discovery_coverage_assertions.statement: Streak is computed as current-consecutive-day-count ending at the most recent contribution day.
discovery_coverage_assertions.scenario: Feed a known calendar pattern to computeCurrentStreak.
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Result matches the trailing-consecutive-days calculation per A6 / IP2 default.

discovery_coverage_assertions.statement: Most-used language is bytes-weighted across owned non-fork repos.
discovery_coverage_assertions.scenario: Feed repos with known byte counts including a fork; compute language.
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Fork bytes are excluded; max-byte language wins.

discovery_coverage_assertions.statement: 90-day visualization shows daily contribution counts for trailing 90 days ending at lookup date.
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: 91 rect cells (13 weeks x 7 days, covering >= 90 days).

discovery_coverage_assertions.statement: Network calls go direct from browser to api.github.com (no proxy).
discovery_coverage_assertions.scenario: Monitor DevTools Network tab during a lookup.
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: All requests target api.github.com; no other origin involved.

discovery_coverage_assertions.statement: No persistence; PAT and username are in-memory only.
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Zero matches (no persistence APIs used).

discovery_coverage_assertions.statement: Nonexistent username shows a clear 'user not found' message.
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Status region shows the documented UserNotFoundError UI message.

discovery_coverage_assertions.statement: PAT auth path is implemented as the default; PAT input is in the UI with explanation and link.
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: #pat-input exists; nearby anchor links to github.com/settings/tokens.

discovery_coverage_assertions.statement: Streak chosen = current streak; UI label literally says 'Current streak'.
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Text includes 'Current streak'.

discovery_coverage_assertions.statement: Most-used language uses bytes-weighted aggregation excluding forks.
discovery_coverage_assertions.scenario: Pass mixed repos to computeMostUsedLanguage including a fork.
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Fork excluded; bytes-weighted winner returned.

discovery_coverage_assertions.statement: 90-day chart sources from GraphQL contributionsCollection.contributionCalendar; label honestly reads 'Contribution activity (last 90 days)' rather than 'commits only'.
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Text includes 'Contribution activity (last 90 days)'.

discovery_coverage_assertions.statement: Visualization form is heatmap strip (13 weeks x 7 days).
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Count === 91.

discovery_coverage_assertions.statement: Card uses GitHub-native dark theme styling with system-ui font.
discovery_coverage_assertions.scenario: Inspect background color of body and font-family.
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Background is a GitHub-dark color (e.g., #0d1117 or #161b22); font-family includes system-ui.

discovery_coverage_assertions.statement: Tool targets github.com (not GitHub Enterprise, not a fork).
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: All API URLs use api.github.com host; zero other GitHub-flavored hosts.

discovery_coverage_assertions.statement: Input is a GitHub login handle (string), used as the {username} path segment in REST and the login: argument in GraphQL.
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: username variable substituted into /users/{login} and user(login: "...") query.

discovery_coverage_assertions.statement: Pinned repos are sourced from GraphQL user.pinnedItems(types: [REPOSITORY]).
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Body contains 'pinnedItems' selection with types: [REPOSITORY] filter.

discovery_coverage_assertions.statement: Contribution streak is derived client-side from contributionCalendar.weeks[].contributionDays[].
discovery_coverage_assertions.scenario: Grep data-derivers.js for computeCurrentStreak; confirm input is the calendar day array.
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Function signature consumes day array as documented.

discovery_coverage_assertions.statement: Most-used language uses GitHub Linguist byte-counts via REST /repos/{owner}/{repo}/languages.
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Per-repo /languages requests hit api.github.com for each non-fork owned repo.

discovery_coverage_assertions.statement: 90-day commit activity is derived from GraphQL contributionsCollection.contributionCalendar sliced to trailing 90 days.
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Heatmap covers 91 cells / 13 weeks ending at lookup date.

discovery_coverage_assertions.statement: Opening index.html in a modern desktop browser on Windows renders the UI without console errors.
discovery_coverage_assertions.scenario: Double-click output/final/index.html in Windows Explorer, or open via local static server. Open DevTools Console.
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Console has zero uncaught errors on initial load.

discovery_coverage_assertions.statement: Page presents an obvious input for the GitHub username and a way to submit it.
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: #username-input and #lookup-button both visible above the fold.

discovery_coverage_assertions.statement: PAT field is present and labeled with explanation and PAT-creation link.
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: #pat-input present (type=password), label/explanation text nearby, anchor link to github.com/settings/tokens.

discovery_coverage_assertions.statement: Valid username + valid PAT renders card with avatar, name, handle, pinned repos, streak, language, and 90-day chart.
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: All five card elements rendered with their respective data.

discovery_coverage_assertions.statement: Nonexistent username shows clear 'user not found' message.
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Status region shows the UserNotFoundError mapped message.

discovery_coverage_assertions.statement: Missing PAT shows either graceful degradation OR a clear 'PAT required' message — never an unexplained blank.
discovery_coverage_assertions.scenario: Submit a username with the PAT field empty.
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Either degraded card with explanation, or 'PAT required' message. Either way, no silent blank.

discovery_coverage_assertions.statement: Rate limit or bad PAT produces actionable error message, not silent blank.
discovery_coverage_assertions.scenario: Submit with a deliberately bad PAT.
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: AuthError-mapped message appears.

discovery_coverage_assertions.statement: User with zero pinned repos renders an empty-state 'No pinned repositories' message, not a broken section.
discovery_coverage_assertions.scenario: Look up a user known to have no pinned repos.
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Pinned section displays the documented empty-state text.

discovery_coverage_assertions.statement: Each metric on the card self-labels with an unambiguous definition.
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Card text contains 'Current streak', 'Most-used language', and 'Contribution activity (last 90 days)'.

discovery_coverage_assertions.statement: Second lookup updates the card without page reload.
discovery_coverage_assertions.scenario: Do two lookups in sequence with different usernames.
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Card updates in place; no full page reload.

discovery_coverage_assertions.statement: No user authentication flow for the tool itself (no accounts, no login).
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Zero matches.

discovery_coverage_assertions.statement: No server-side component, hosted backend, or proxy.
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: No server files (.py/.js Node/.go), no Procfile, no Dockerfile, no proxy configuration.

discovery_coverage_assertions.statement: No database or persistent storage of looked-up users.
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Zero matches.

discovery_coverage_assertions.statement: No multi-user comparison UI.
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Exactly one card per lookup; no compare panel.

discovery_coverage_assertions.statement: No private-repository or private-contribution data shown.
discovery_coverage_assertions.scenario: Grep js/ for 'isPrivate' true-path or visibility filters allowing private.
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Public-only filter or no private-repo handling in the UI.

discovery_coverage_assertions.statement: Tool is read-only; no follow, star, or issue creation.
discovery_coverage_assertions.scenario: Grep js/ for 'PUT', 'POST', 'PATCH', 'DELETE' against api.github.com excluding the single GraphQL POST.
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Only the GraphQL POST is non-GET; no mutation REST calls.

discovery_coverage_assertions.statement: Tool targets user accounts, not organizations.
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Either renders graceful 'not a user account' message OR renders with documented limitations; does not crash.

discovery_coverage_assertions.statement: Tool targets github.com only, not GitHub Enterprise Server.
discovery_coverage_assertions.scenario: Grep js/ for API host URLs.
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: All requests go to api.github.com; no configurable endpoint.

discovery_coverage_assertions.statement: Desktop-browser primary target; mobile-specific layout not in scope.
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Zero or minimal media queries; no separate mobile layout.

discovery_coverage_assertions.statement: No image-export, share link, or embed feature.
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: No 'share', 'export', 'download', or 'embed' button.

discovery_coverage_assertions.statement: No notifications, scheduled refresh, or background polling.
discovery_coverage_assertions.scenario: Grep js/ for setInterval, setTimeout > 1000, notification API.
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: No long-running timers, no Notification API usage.

discovery_coverage_assertions.statement: No internationalization or multi-language UI.
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Zero matches.

files: js/main.js (ui-shell wiring + entry point loaded via <script type='module'>)
external_runtime_dependencies: api.github.com (GraphQL + REST) — intrinsic to the prompt; the only acceptable runtime network dependency
